description_long: This is a ceramic teapot with a rounded body, a gently curved spout, and a large loop handle. The exterior is decorated with a repeating geometric pattern of concentric circles in blue, white, and yellow, interspersed with vertical teal leaf motifs. The teapot features a matching lid with a small knob, also adorned with the same circular pattern. The spout is slightly curved and tapers to a small opening, while the handle is thick and ergonomically shaped for easy gripping. The surface has a matte to semi-gloss finish, giving it a traditional, handcrafted appearance. The overall design suggests a functional yet decorative piece suitable for serving tea.
description: A rounded ceramic teapot with blue and white geometric patterns, a curved spout, and a loop handle. It features a matching patterned lid and a matte to semi-gloss finish.
description_view_0: The teapot is shown from a side angle, highlighting the curved spout on the left and the large loop handle on the right, with the decorative patterns clearly visible.
description_view_1: This view presents the teapot from the front, directly facing the spout, with the lid and circular motifs prominently displayed.
description_view_2: The teapot is viewed from the opposite side of view 0, showing the handle on the left and the spout on the right, with the patterned body in full view.
description_view_3: The teapot is seen from the back, focusing on the handle and the rear curvature of the body, with the lid and patterns still visible.
attribute_spout_orientation: The spout is gently curved, angled slightly upward, and positioned at the front of the teapot body.
attribute_handle_design: The handle is a thick, rounded loop attached at the upper and lower rear of the teapot, providing an ergonomic grip.
attribute_lid_presence: Present; the lid is domed with a small central knob and fits snugly on top.
attribute_decorative_patterns: Geometric concentric circles in blue, white, and yellow, with vertical teal leaf motifs encircling the body and lid.
